payload.description: Discover active control planes, control towers, reporting systems, and execution gaps across AWS, Supabase, and GitHub so named systems can be verified against real runtime evidence.

checks.name: supabase_public_tables
checks.query: select table_schema, table_name from information_schema.tables where table_schema in ('public') order by table_schema, table_name

checks.name: supabase_recent_runtime_tables
checks.query: candidate runtime ledger tables and audit surfaces likely related to control plane, command centre, task queues, runs, telemetry, evidence, control, bridge, lambda, workflow, scheduler, status, and execution

checks.name: github_bridge_paths
checks.query: scan repository for bridge runner workflow paths, payload folders, dispatch triggers, and runner pickup conventions

detect: systems that exist but have zero or near-zero runtime evidence
scheduled jobs defined but not firing
lambdas deployed but never invoked recently
supabase entities likely intended as control or reporting surfaces but not active
names that imply control plane, control tower, command centre, telemetry, scheduler, audit, probe, orchestration, or bridge but lack proof
analysis.goal: Identify where the real control plane is, what the command centre is only rendering, and which named systems should now be made actually operational because the bridge runner is working.
